rationale: Question asks 'what architectural feature causes variance' — diagnosis of P99 spike to big.LITTLE. Fix is well-known affinity pin, not design. Napkin math is explanatory, not design trade-off.
Designs custom op acceleration approach across NPU/DSP stack with low-level kernel development choices; open-ended architecture design.
Scenario asks 'what sync mechanism did you forget' — clear root-cause question. Answer is a specific well-known fix (fence). Napkin math quantifies symptom, not design trade-offs.
Compound: diagnose three bias sources AND redesign federated selection with stratified sampling and inverse propensity. Could be mastery but design is defensible.
Designs full on-device grounding architecture with handoff protocol, memory budget computed. Clear design@L6+.
Architects vector store + embedding pipeline + LDP mechanism with concrete memory and latency sizing. Textbook design@L6+.
Designs model architecture + context mgmt + quantization strategy under tight RAM/latency. Design@L6+ fits.
Design a sync protocol with multiple options (delta, soft-labels, hybrid) and budget calculations. Design@L6+ defensible.
Design adaptive inference strategy under thermal throttling with multi-tier models and transition logic.
Design distributed inference partitioning + comm protocol + overhead analysis on three MCUs. Design@L6+ clearly fits.
Core task is hypothesis + root cause (heap fragmentation); fix is standard pre-allocation. Not a new design, diagnosis@L5 fits better than design@L6+.
Designs federated learning under extreme LoRaWAN constraints: sparse gradient protocol, energy budget, duty cycle. Design@L6+ canonical.
Designs two-path interrupt-driven pipeline with fast matched filter + slow ML classifier and precise latency budget. Design@L6+ fits.
Designs solar/supercap power budget from physics with voltage-triggered duty cycling. Design@L6+ canonical.
Designs tiered power architecture with always-on vs triggered sensors + fusion model + 5-day budget. Design@L6+ fits.
Question asks 'can the ESP32-S3 handle this and what are bottlenecks' — feasibility analysis with utilization numbers, not a design. analyze@L5 fits.
Designs complete keyword spotting pipeline from raw audio to classification with streaming memory budget. Design@L6+.
Asks what constraints must be encoded in NAS search space — specification/enumeration of constraints rather than full design of the NAS system itself.
Designs constrained NAS search space to meet 4-GPU-hour budget with two-stage strategy. Design@L6+ defensible.
Designs sub-milliwatt wake-word architecture with tiered DSP + MCU duty-cycling, full power budget. Design@L6+.
Classic race-condition debug: 'how did the lock cause deadlock?' — diagnosis of non-atomic RMW. Fix is hardware spinlock. Not a new design.
Root-cause DMA/CPU race; fix is standard ping-pong buffer pattern. Primarily diagnosis though it includes design of the fix.
Derives energy break-even formula for DMA vs CPU copy — quantitative design with explicit equation formulation. Design@L6+ defensible.
Designs mixed-precision strategy with layer-wise analysis based on dynamic range. Design@L6+ fits.
Designs three-layer on-device fault detection within MCU resource and LoRa uplink constraints. Design@L6+ canonical.
Designs Flash-paged KV-cache architecture with explicit tier hierarchy and latency tradeoff. Design@L6+ fits.
Designs unbrickable OTA with immutable bootloader + A/B partitioning + commit-confirm handshake within 1MB flash. Design@L6+ canonical.
Designs hierarchical Look-aside Cache for ViT over 50 frames with memory-tier awareness. Design@L6+ fits.
Designs paired-watchdog hot-reload with CRC integrity + timing budget for automotive redundancy. Design@L6+ fits.
Designs multi-level memory hierarchy with sliding window + DMA tiling. Proves naive impossible (16x over). Design@L6+ fits.
Designs multi-layered on-device defense against silent data drift with circuit breaker + fallback model. Design@L6+.
Re-architects inference scheduler with time-sliced shared arena for concurrent KWS. Design@L6+ fits.
Synthesizes business economics ($50M TCO), physical viability (2.975mJ/round), and FL design — all four skills: recall, analyze, design, quantify. Mastery per heuristic.
TCO quantification (365TB vs 3.65TB) + energy analysis + hybrid architecture design. Compound recall+analyze+create+quantify → mastery.
Root-cause of quantization-calibration mismatch under noisy audio; fix is mixed-precision for first 3 layers. Diagnosis + fix, not greenfield design.
ROI proposal: physical viability (84KB, 5.95s/round, 0.298J) + battery budget + FL design. Four-skill compound → mastery.
Designs paged KV-cache with Flash-as-L2 swap + DMA orchestration for 480KB SRAM. Design@L6+ canonical.
Diagnose side-channel + multi-phase incident response + TCO ($500M recall vs $180 OTA) + DP design. Mastery compound per heuristic.
Phased mixed-precision plan for AEB pedestrian detection to address calibration mismatch. Design@L6+ fits.
Multi-layered defense-in-depth sensor occlusion detection with 10µs overhead budget within 1ms deadline. Design@L6+ fits.
TCO calc ($12.2M vs $6.2M) + security risk analysis + hybrid architecture. Business+physical+security synthesis → mastery per heuristic.
Designs self-sufficient resilient system addressing flash endurance + pathological outputs. Design@L6+.
'Walk me through the math' of integer Conv2D — this is mechanical recall+quantification of the quantized arithmetic pipeline, not design. fluency@L4.
Designs on-device incremental learning with last-layer fine-tune + memory/energy budget vs satellite TCO. Design@L6+ defensible.
Designs hardware-aware NAS+compression AutoML across 5 MCU targets with TCO. Design@L6+ canonical.
Quantitative airtime/duty-cycle calculation (53ms × 3000/hr, 1% limit) and channel spread. More fluency/quantification than open design. Level L4 fits.
Compute hops (12), latency (180-300ms) vs 500ms budget, grid topology — quantitative sizing with standard formula, not open design. fluency@L4.
Classic 'identify training-serving skew' — short root-cause recall. Diagnosis@L2 appropriate.
Single-step DNS TTL caching recall. Diagnosis@L2 clearly correct.
Diagnoses non-linear collapse via queueing theory + GPU memory fragmentation + comparison to CPU. Diagnosis@L5 defensible; has quant and create aspects but root-cause dominates.
Root-cause why chunked prefill raises TTFT — arithmetic intensity collapse and 25x weight reloads. Quantitative diagnosis@L5.
Root-cause OOM from speculative decoding: 4x memory amplification due to [batch,k,dim] query tensors. Diagnosis@L5 fits.
Explains prefill-blocking causing TTFT regression; critiques wrong playbook advice. Diagnosis@L5 canonical.
Root-cause why priority queue fails — atomic GPU iterations can't be preempted. Diagnosis@L5 correct.
Explains scheduler-induced HoL with long-prompt prefill dominating. Diagnosis@L5 defensible.
Diagnoses deadline-oblivious FIFO death spiral with queueing math. Solution requires deadline-aware scheduling. Diagnosis@L5.
Queueing death spiral diagnosis with explicit λ/μ/ρ analysis and memory-bound decode rate. Diagnosis@L5 fits.
Identify KV cache as OOM cause + mitigation via PagedAttention. Standard diagnosis@L5.
MIG shared L2/HBM BW diagnosis with roofline-ridge analysis. Diagnosis@L5 defensible.
KV-cache OOM attack root-cause + emergency response; 64x amplification sizing. Diagnosis@L5 fits.
Arithmetic intensity diagnosis: BS=1 memory-bound vs BS=64 compute-bound; speculative decoding hurts. Diagnosis@L5.
Diagnoses replicated activations at 128k seq dominating; requires Sequence Parallelism. Diagnosis@L5 fits.
Cross-node NVSwitch→IB BW diagnosis with per-layer AllReduce costing. Diagnosis@L5 defensible.
'Design a data quality pipeline' — explicit 4-layer architecture spec with detection latency budget. Design-dominant, not diagnosis.
Explains FlashAttention speedup via HBM traffic reduction (258GB→much less). Bytes-moved diagnosis@L5.
Diagnoses speculative decoding collapse under saturation — compute opportunity cost. Diagnosis@L5 canonical.
Energy-movement invariant: DRAM access 200x MAC; unstructured pruning doesn't save energy. Diagnosis@L5.
Memory coalescing diagnosis with 4/128 utilization; 30x slowdown explained. Diagnosis@L5 fits.
KV-cache fragmentation root-cause; PagedAttention is fix. Diagnosis@L5 canonical.
Embedding hotspot diagnosis: hash sharding + gradient accumulation on hot shard during flash sale. Diagnosis@L5 fits.
ZeRO-1 only shards optimizer state; weights+gradients still replicated (120GB > 80GB). Diagnosis@L5.
Prefill (compute-bound) blocks Decode (memory-bound) on single pool; disaggregation is fix. Diagnosis@L5 fits.
Contiguous pre-allocation causes internal fragmentation (99.4% waste); PagedAttention fix. Diagnosis@L5 fits.
cron logrotate stalling fsync → blocking AllReduce across 512 GPUs. Straggler root-cause. Diagnosis@L5.
Correctable ECC rate elevated causing memory controller stalls; reproducibility threshold analysis. Diagnosis@L5 fits.
'Design a data quality pipeline' with contracts, schema validation, lineage — an architecture spec with quantitative validation budget. Design@L6+.
'Evaluate trade-offs' between full vs selective checkpointing with quantitative memory (70→15GB) and FLOP (33%→15%) comparison. Evaluation@L5 per heuristic.
Evaluates three defenses quantitatively (cost, utility) against model extraction attack. Evaluation@L5 fits better than diagnosis.
Root-cause of BN instability at micro-batch 1, then replace with GN/IN. Diagnosis+fix. Diagnosis@L5 defensible though evaluation@L5 possible.
Evaluates decontamination pipeline with MinHash LSH; quantifies leakage (60GB→flagged 0.1% vs 2%). Evaluation@L5 fits.
Evaluates Team A vs B vs PagedAttention with latency/compute numbers (20ms sync, 16x FLOPs). Rejects both, proposes third. Evaluation@L5.
Evaluates centralized checkpointing (384s > 120s SLA) vs distributed with Lustre. Quant comparison of alternatives. Evaluation@L5.
Root-cause PFC congestion spread; DCQCN fix with ECN threshold math. Diagnosis@L5 defensible.
Evaluates Blue/Green vs Canary vs In-Place against VRAM budget (15+15>24GB); picks Canary. Evaluation@L5 fits better.
Evaluates CPU pinning/NUMA trade-offs (cache hit 15ns vs cross-NUMA 300ns); bounds throughput vs P99. Evaluation@L5.
Evaluates row-level vs partition-level lineage with quantitative 5B events vs metadata-only. Chooses latter. Evaluation@L5.
Evaluates dynamic batching config against SLA with precise worst-case math (60+50+20=130>100). Derives correct timeout. Evaluation@L5.
Evaluates EF memory OOM trade-off (40GB dense vs 5GB sharded FP16) and justifies ZeRO-2 sharding. Evaluation@L5.
Evaluates compacting GC vs paged allocator quantitatively (30% fragmentation→0% external). Evaluation@L5.
Evaluates standard+checkpointing vs FlashAttention at 32k ctx (68.7GB→~268MB, 250x). Evaluation@L5 fits.
Evaluates three power measurement techniques (1Hz poll, PDU, on-chip counters) for µs-resolution ground truth. Evaluation@L5.
Evaluates hedge threshold at P95 (10ms) vs amplification (+5%); quantifies 150ms→15ms improvement vs 20ms SLA. Evaluation@L5.
Evaluates Redis-coordinated idempotency vs deterministic HMAC with 5B op latency comparison. Chooses stateless. Evaluation@L5.
Evaluates static KV allocation feasibility (512GB>260GB), proposes PagedAttention. Quant comparison. Evaluation@L5.
Evaluates FP16 (687GB) vs INT8 KV quantization (343GB) vs 500GB budget; asymmetric K/V quantization strategy. Evaluation@L5.
Root-cause of BN at batch=2 instability + LayerNorm replacement. Diagnosis-dominant though has evaluation elements. Diagnosis@L5 defensible.
Evaluates dynamic vs static vs bucketed memory planning with quantitative OOM/waste trade-offs. Picks bucketed power-of-2. Evaluation@L5.
Evaluates cardinality explosion (25M series × 3KB = 75GB) and proposes label reduction + traces. Tradeoff analysis. Evaluation@L5.
Evaluates offline MMLU vs production code: sequence error compounds 99.5%→36.7% over 200 tokens. Evaluation@L5.
Evaluates multiple-testing correction: 92.3% FP rate with α=0.05, Bonferroni vs BH trade-offs. Evaluation@L5 fits.
Evaluates NUMA asymmetry with xGMI BW (72GB/s) vs per-socket demand (128GB/s) and justifies thread pinning redesign. Evaluation@L5.
Quantifies 36%→99% memory utilization gain with PagedAttention blocks; evaluation of alternative. Evaluation@L5 fits.
Quantifies 87.5% internal fragmentation (4GB vs 0.5GB), justifies paged dynamic allocator. Evaluation@L5.
Evaluates three alternatives (TP, FlashAttention, sliding) with memory math (8.7TB vs budget). Picks FA2+checkpoint. Evaluation@L5.
Evaluates bin-packing vs right-sizing; quantifies CPU:GPU ratio mismatch (24 vs 16). Evaluation@L5 fits.
Evaluates invariant checks vs diagnostics vs DMR for SDC; SDC MTBF math (53% per interval). Evaluation@L5.
Evaluates three proxy metric strategies for delayed-label drift; MMD on embeddings justified quantitatively. Evaluation@L5.
Evaluates impact of skip connection pinning on activation lifetime; justifies checkpointing. Quant 268MB pin analysis. Evaluation@L5.
Evaluates full activation logging (650GB/s) vs linear probes (2000x reduction). Quantitative tradeoff choice. Evaluation@L5.
Explains three reproducibility gaps (compute, inference, accuracy) with root causes. Diagnosis@L5 fits.
Systematic feature-pipeline debugging with KL divergence + root-cause of zeroed feature. Diagnosis@L5 canonical.
Three causes of stream overlap failure (default stream, PCIe contention, small transfers). Diagnosis@L5.
GPU fragmentation diagnosis + three-level fix (admission, compaction, elastic). Diagnosis@L5 with design elements; defensible.
Data gravity diagnosis: 1600TB × epochs / 50GB/s = 8.9hrs/epoch. Propose Lustre caching. Diagnosis@L5 defensible.
Producer-consumer imbalance in NVMe staging + Lustre drain lag causing disk fill. Diagnosis@L5 fits.
Synchronous barrier amplification: single 500ms I/O stall → 7200 GPU-s loss. Diagnosis@L5.
Evaluates where MLIR retargetability breaks (tiling, TMA, libraries); quantifies 40% gap composition. Evaluation@L5.
Caching allocator fragmentation root-cause: 15GB in 3000×5MB blocks; no 20GB contiguous. Diagnosis@L5 canonical.
Evaluates silent fairness degradation mechanisms and designs continuous fairness monitoring. Both diagnosis+design → evaluation@L5.
TPU MXU padding root-cause: 150→256, 1000→1024, 500→512 = 44% waste. Diagnosis@L5 fits.
FP8 MoE alignment + Amax launch overhead diagnosis; explains regression. Diagnosis@L5 fits.
Root-cause: EP during decode wastes BW loading full 8B/GPU at batch=1; TP is fix. Diagnosis@L5.
Implicit KV broadcast materialization from 65→524MB in HBM negates GQA benefit. Diagnosis@L5 canonical.
NUMA/UPI BW saturation explains asymmetric GPU throughput (1500 vs 700 img/s). Classic diagnosis@L5.
ACS/IOMMU blocks P2P; NCCL falls back to PCIe 24GB/s vs NVLink 300GB/s. Classic diagnosis@L5.
RDMA incast → PFC storm root-cause with buffer math (25.5MB vs 32MB). Classic diagnosis@L5.
NVSwitch port contention from MoE load imbalance; GPU 0 ingress saturation. Diagnosis@L5 canonical.
Pageable memory bounce buffer root-cause with 30ms pinned-alloc overhead under fragmentation. Diagnosis@L5.
/dev/shm 64MB default breaks NCCL IPC, forcing small-chunk fallback (300→20GB/s). Classic diagnosis@L5.
80GB checkpoint D2H saturates PCIe Gen4 x16 (20GB/s), starves dataloader H2D for 4s. Diagnosis@L5.
DCQCN slow start misconfig throttles short AllReduce bursts; utilization math (0.2ms→80ms). Diagnosis@L5.
CXL latency-bound thrashing with MLP=2 limited random fetches; 125ms vs 2ms bandwidth-limited. Diagnosis@L5.
Default stream serializes cudaMemcpyAsync with compute; 15.6+30ms vs overlapped max. Classic diagnosis@L5.
Shared switch buffer + lack of QoS isolates RDMA; TCP 40Gbps fills 16MB buffer → 1.28ms/hop. Diagnosis@L5.
Activation outliers in 13B+ models destroy W8A8 quantization; SmoothQuant fix. Root-cause diagnosis@L5.
FP8 E4M3 router overflow (max 448) causes softmax collapse to one-hot; swap to E5M2 for router. Diagnosis@L5.
Attention sinks + INT8 per-tensor quant destroys non-sink token precision at long ctx. Diagnosis@L5 fits.
STE impedance mismatch: weight update 0.15 > INT4 delta 0.133 causes bin jumps. Diagnosis@L5.
GPTQ Hessian overfits to C4 English/Python calibration → Java dims get zero weight. Diagnosis@L5.
Unfused dequant adds 60GB write + 60GB read roundtrip erasing BW savings. Diagnosis@L5.
Roofline crossover: AI=2 at B=1 vs AI=256 at B=128 crosses machine balance 156. W4A16 hurts compute-bound. Diagnosis@L5.
FP8 doubles compute ceiling but decode is memory-bound at BS=1; only the 2x BW reduction helps. Diagnosis@L5.
Depthwise conv AI=0.5 is memory-bound; A10G 33% lower BW → slower despite 92% more TFLOPS. Diagnosis@L5.
Vertical fusion of embedding+MLP serializes MXUs behind sparse gathers; breaks pipeline parallelism. Diagnosis@L5.
RoPE table thrashes TLB (65.5MB); recompute eliminates scatter reads and uses idle FLOPs. Diagnosis@L5.
Prefill is compute-bound (QK^T FLOPs unchanged by GQA); only decode benefits from KV BW reduction. Diagnosis@L5.
CSR SpMM falls back from Tensor Cores (312 TF) to CUDA cores (19 TF) + irregular access lowers AI. Diagnosis@L5.
Batch 32 hits roofline ridge; beyond that compute-bound → throughput flat, latency grows linearly. Diagnosis@L5.
AI=1 << ridge 138 means BS=1 is BW-bound; pruning FLOPs without structured reduces nothing. Diagnosis@L5.
Prefill AI 2048 >> ridge 156 is already compute-bound; batching adds no throughput. Classic diagnosis@L5.
